title: Broadcasting Dashboards
excerpt: Broadcasting is a great feature that we currently use with the free RiseVision app on an Amazon Fire Stick to display our boards in our office TV. I love the battery widget on the dashboard and would love to be able to b…

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml: Broadcasting is a great feature that we currently use with the free RiseVision app on an Amazon Fire Stick to display our boards in our office TV. I love the battery widget on the dashboard and would love to be able to broadcast that on the TV as well. We would use this for specific boards (events) as well as overall performance (weekly sprints).

posts.author: Qassam-monday.com (Qassam Atme)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @rick

The Broadcast view is still exclusive for boards only But I’d love to understand the need to broadcast a dashboard.

Plus, why won’t you screenplay the dashboard itself?

Let me know

Cheers!

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml:
As I mentioned, we have a TV in our office that we display Monday.com boards on. We are in event management so we will typically have the boards for the next event or two up on the TV. We use the free RiseVision digital signage app to embed the iframe and rotate through the boards.

Without tying up a computer, or purchasing another media device, we have no other way to display a dashboard on the TV.

Dashboards are great for customized visualizations. If we had the ability to display dashboards we could use the battery widget for weekly sprints, the time widget for event countdowns, the numbers widget to make sure we are on budget, and the llama widget just because it’s fun.

posts.author: Galit-monday.com (Galit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Wow @rick I love this idea! I believe we actually have this in the pipeline to broadcast dashboards so please stay tuned for this! I can’t give you an exact time but I will definitely share this with our product team!

(We also love and use our llama widget )

posts.author: TheOtherPM (Paul McGregor)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d also love to see more functionality added to the Board broadcasts. E.g. Choose whether to include Updates or not. Choose to enable people to add to some columns but not others. Allow people to add updates. And so on.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the additional feedback @TheOtherPM. Might be cool to open a new thread for this though as think it covers another separate feedback on broadcast view, not connected to dashboards! Thanks!

posts.author: jacobazimmer (Jacob Zimmerman)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this. Having a broadcast view for dashboards would be great! I am using ProPresenter 6 in our office to scroll through our boards, but I would love to have more functionality and customization to my display

posts.author: Qassam-monday.com (Qassam Atme)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jacobazimmer - First, welcome to the monday community

Let us know if you have any further feedback, we’d love to pass your input to our product team

posts.author: V.B.Music (Mike  Alducin)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you are using Google Chrome, you can cast anything from your browser through Chrome Cast. I guess there must be another functions such as this one in other browsers.
We sometimes cast the dashboard, the maps view and other things in the reception of the office this way

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml: @V.B.Music This is true but requires a computer. The broadcast feature can be embedded into websites for public facing information as well as into digital signage systems without the need for a dedicated computer attached to one display. That is what we are after with this request.

posts.author: nick (Nick)
posts.cookedHtml: We provide our clients with Data Studio Dashboards that show them information from a range of different sources (accounting system re invoices, time tracking system for monthly budget). It would be great to be able to embed Monday boards into their dashboard too.

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml:
We currently investigating Google Data Studio for our clients as well. Embedding a broadcasted dashboard would be next level.

I hope the Monday.com team has this in the pipeline.

posts.author: TekkGroupCharlie (Charlie Garnish)
posts.cookedHtml: We would like this too, i have old thikpads resign behind a tv in the warehouse at the moment

posts.author: jackjack2202 (Jack Kubicek)
posts.cookedHtml: Wanted to put my thoughts in as well. We are working on a monday.com app that would work great with the ability to also have a dashboard inside of it! Dont think it would make sense for us to take the time to create our own metrics. Would love to hear an update if this ever made it up the chain.

posts.author: Concept (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
We wanted to use the dashboard same way.
The problem is the low limitation of the amount of boards …
So for now the dashboards are NOT useful for us at all.

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you’re looking for a way to display Monday data and metrics on a TV, you might want to take a look at Screenful. It pulls data from the Monday API and creates a visual dashboard which you can set on rotation on a TV screen.

You can learn more at Monday.com Dashboards: All the Monday.com metrics you need to make better decisions! — Screenful

We also wrote a blog post about the process of setting up the hardware. Check out the tips regarding the suggested devices - we’ve tried many hardwares and they have all failed miserably, including Android mini PCs, and Raspberry Pi:s. Be warned, most hardware won’t be able to run 24/7 without interruption.

Screenful

Displaying dashboard on a TV: Setting up the hardware — Screenful

Ever since we started developing our analytics dashboard product, we've been interested not only about the software but also the hardware part. We've played around with different hardware to come up with reliable yet affordable setup. Basically what...

posts.author: SarahDhoo (Sarah Donahoo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jumping in late here I know (!), but sharing since it looks like people are still interested in workarounds until Monday releases a native feature for this.

Fugo.ai has feature a that enables sharing of dashboard links - or any password or SSO protected URL - on TV screens. You can find it on their “TV Dashboard” page on their website.

It works by logging into your Monday account and taking screenshots of your board that auto-refresh on screens without any manual intervention on your end. It runs on pretty much any consumer or pro-grade hardware running Android, Windows, ChromeOS + a few dedicated digital signage hardware platforms (think BrightSign, IAdea.)

So if you’ve got something like a Chromecast or Raspberry Pi laying around the office, you can use it to set up your dashboard broadcasts pretty quickly & easily.

Hope this helps some people out!
